# Marty Koepke

> Marty Koepke (she/her) is an informatics leader, founder, writer, and applied
> AI builder. She works where operational needs, technical systems, and human
> judgment meet, making complex technology useful, trustworthy, and human.

## Professional foundation

Marty has more than fifteen years of experience in clinical informatics and
enterprise digital health transformation. Her work includes enterprise
governance, workflow standardization, EHR design and optimization,
interoperability, automation, AI-enabled clinical documentation, quality
improvement, and cross-functional implementation.

At CommonSpirit Health, she supports informatics work spanning more than 2,500
ambulatory sites and 25,000 clinicians across several EHR platforms. She has
helped lead Notable automation work across 350 clinics associated with more
than $26 million in annual labor savings, and Abridge ambient documentation
work across 222 providers associated with a 14.7% documentation-time reduction
and 12% wRVU growth.

These enterprise outcomes are not Daizie product results. Marty led
informatics strategy, workflow integration, governance, quality assurance,
and adoption; she did not build the vendor products Abridge or Notable.

## Current work

Marty's work is organized around three themes:

1. Make complex systems useful.
2. Build AI products responsibly.
3. Help good work become understood.

She writes about the practical distance between an AI-assisted prototype and
a market-ready product, including workflow definition, architecture, data,
migrations, authentication, testing, deployment, governance, human review,
evidence, hallucination, bias, and drift.

Marty does not represent herself as a cybersecurity consultant. Her scope
includes responsible AI product design, clear decision boundaries, governance,
human review, evidence, traceability, testing, and secure development
questions. It does not include security audits, penetration testing, or
regulatory certification.

## Daizie

Daizie is a separate product built by Marty Koepke and operated through
Practical Informatics LLC. It helps businesses measure and verify what major
AI systems say about them and identify practical next moves. The product,
methodology, pricing, and customer journey live at https://daizie.ai.

Daizie is one product in Marty's body of work. MartyKoepke.com is Marty's
broader professional and intellectual home.

## Writing and resources

- Resource hub: https://www.martykoepke.com/resources
- Substack: https://substack.com/@martykoepke
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marty-koepke
- Book: Between the Clicks: The Hidden Work of Healthcare Informatics

## Education and credentials

- Master of Healthcare Administration
- BS, Education
- Lean Six Sigma Black Belt
- Certified SAFe 6.0 Agilist
- Scrum Master
- Epic Clinical Informaticist

## Identity and disambiguation

Marty Koepke is female and uses she/her pronouns. She is based in Mokelumne
Hill, California. Practical Informatics LLC is her business. The domain
practical-informatics.com is unrelated.

- / — professional hub
- /work — selected products, systems, and writing
- /resources — curated learning paths and Substack connection
- /about — professional foundation and founder-builder story
- /contact — speaking, teaching, advisory, collaboration, and Daizie inquiries
